overview: TestFlow consists of a single independent component with no dependencies, so all work falls into Phase 1. The component is a self-contained Go web service requiring no external services or databases.

phases.name: Foundation Services
phases.description: Single independent web service with no dependencies

components.name: testflow-web
responsibilities: Serve a server-rendered HTML greeting page with dynamic name personalization via query parameter
Provide a health check endpoint returning JSON status
Sanitize all user input to prevent XSS attacks
Return styled 404 pages for undefined routes
Render accessible, responsive HTML with WCAG 2.1 Level A compliance
components.architectureContext: This is the sole component in the system. It serves as both the frontend and backend, handling HTTP requests directly via Go's net/http. Nothing calls it except end users and infrastructure health checks.
keyConsiderations: Use html.EscapeString() for all user input sanitization before template rendering — no external dependencies allowed (stdlib only)
Multi-stage Docker build: golang:1.22-alpine for build, gcr.io/distroless/static-debian12 for runtime, exposing port 9090
Use html/template (not text/template) for automatic contextual escaping as defense-in-depth
Ensure all form elements have associated labels and keyboard navigability for WCAG compliance
Keep response times under 2 seconds — since there's no DB or external calls, this is straightforward but verify with the distroless image
components.apiContract:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: TestFlow Web
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Simple Hello World web application
paths:
  /:
    get:
      summary: Serve the Hello World page
      parameters:
        - name: name
          in: query
          required: false
          schema:
            type: string
          description: Optional name for personalized greeting
      responses:
        '200':
          description: HTML page with greeting
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
